title: U6: prove live cm compatibility, latency (<4s/8s), and rollback
description:
U6 — Prove live cm compatibility, latency, child cleanup, and rollback. This is the single pre-merge acceptance session.

Plan authority: docs/plans/2026-07-20-002-feat-cass-compat-shim-plan.md, Implementation Unit U6 and Verification Contract.
Owns R17 and AE1-AE8. Dependency: coordinating U5 container. Live consumer behavior is the acceptance authority; do not activate the shim persistently unless every gate is green.

ATOMICITY DECISION — DO NOT SPLIT
U6 is one ordered acceptance transaction over one built artifact, one live corpus, one process baseline, one timing series, and one rollback/config-integrity comparison. Splitting would make latency warm-state, orphan attribution, or rollback evidence ambiguous. Keep all proof in one shell/session and preserve artifacts from any failed gate.

OUTCOME
Demonstrate that U1-U5's built behavior works with the pinned installed cm consumer and live corpus: shim-marked doctor stats, schema-valid canonical search hits, timeline-driven session discovery, accepted export, successful context without degraded output, reflect dry-run without fallback, one cold plus three warm context calls each <4.00s, no new fff-mcp orphan, and environment-only rollback to real cass with unchanged cass-memory config.

PRECONDITIONS / STOP CONDITIONS
- cm --version identifies cm 0.2.12; cass --version identifies real cass 0.6.22.
- dist/cass-shim.js is owner-executable after a clean build.
- At least one parseable real Claude session exists within the timeline window for export/reflect proof.
- The predecessor days/workspace contract and all U1-U5 children are complete.
- Use CASS_PATH only as an env prefix or exported variable inside this acceptance shell. Never write ~/.cass-memory/config.json.
- Stop release sign-off on any wrong version, missing corpus prerequisite, >=4.00s sample, cm timeout, schema/path failure, fallback, UNKNOWN block, new orphan, shim marker after rollback, or config state change. Preserve the acceptance directory and exact failing stdout/stderr/status/timing.

FILES AND DEFECT ROUTING
No file changes by default. At most add a narrowly scoped failing process regression to test/packaging.test.ts if live acceptance exposes an unprotected behavior. Route defects to the existing owner:
- U1: argv, cheap verb, streams, error envelope, dispatcher.
- U2: search schema/path/slug/filter/latency caused by the adapter.
- U3: export parsing/render/fallback.
- U4: timeline/stats/enumeration.
- U5a/U5b: docs or packed-install/capabilities.
Do not create a production module from U6. Do not broaden a latency miss into shared-FFF performance work; record it as candidate follow-up (out of scope) unless evidence proves the shim adapter itself is the cause.

TDD / VERIFICATION DISCIPLINE — RED → GREEN → REVIEW
The public seam is the real cm subprocess contract with CASS_PATH pointed at the built shim. The ordered live commands below are this verification-only bead's test substitute; any failing command is RED evidence.

For a defect that needs automated protection:
1. Add one focused failing test first in the owning named test file (test/cass-shim-argv.test.ts, test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts, test/cass-shim-export.test.ts, test/cass-shim-timeline.test.ts, or test/packaging.test.ts).
2. Run that exact test/title and observe the intended behavioral failure, not setup failure.
3. Make the smallest owner-scoped correction, rerun GREEN, then review/refactor without changing behavior.
4. Rerun this entire U6 transaction from a fresh acceptance directory and process baseline. Never weaken a live gate or raise the latency limit to obtain green.

ACCEPTANCE SHELL SETUP — EXACT COMMANDS
Run from /data/projects/agent-session-search in one shell:

  set -u
  test "$PWD" = /data/projects/agent-session-search
  cm --version | tee /tmp/cass-shim-cm-version.txt
  cass --version | tee /tmp/cass-shim-real-cass-version.txt
  rg -q '0\.2\.12' /tmp/cass-shim-cm-version.txt
  rg -q '0\.6\.22' /tmp/cass-shim-real-cass-version.txt
  accept_dir=$(mktemp -d)
  printf '%s\n' "$accept_dir"
  config_path="$HOME/.cass-memory/config.json"
  if test -f "$config_path"; then sha256sum "$config_path" | awk '{print $1}' > "$accept_dir/config.before"; else printf 'ABSENT\n' > "$accept_dir/config.before"; fi
  ps -C fff-mcp -o pid= 2>/dev/null | awk '{$1=$1; if ($1 != "") print $1}' | sort -n > "$accept_dir/fff.before"
  env -u CASS_PATH cm doctor > "$accept_dir/real-doctor.before.stdout" 2> "$accept_dir/real-doctor.before.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  ! rg -q 'agent-session-search-cass-shim' "$accept_dir/real-doctor.before.stdout" "$accept_dir/real-doctor.before.stderr"

Expected: every command exits 0; version files pin exactly the plan baseline; accept_dir is printed for evidence retention; config.before is either one SHA-256 or ABSENT; fff.before is a PID baseline; default cm doctor succeeds without a shim marker. If default cassPath already points at the shim, stop: rollback cannot be proven with environment-only activation.

AUTOMATED PRE-GATE — EXACT COMMANDS
  npm run check
  npx vitest run test/cass-shim-argv.test.ts test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts test/cass-shim-export.test.ts test/cass-shim-timeline.test.ts
  npx vitest run test/packaging.test.ts test/readme.test.ts test/mcp-smoke.test.ts test/native-mcp-smoke.test.ts
  npm test
  npm run build
  npm run smoke
  test -x dist/cass-shim.js
  test -f dist/cass-compat/run.js

Expected: every command exits 0; targeted/full suites report no failed tests; build succeeds; shim is executable and nested dispatcher exists. Any failure stops live acceptance.

BUILT-BINARY GATES — EXACT COMMANDS AND EXPECTED OUTCOMES
1. Timed cheap version path:
  /usr/bin/time -f '%e' -o "$accept_dir/version.time" dist/cass-shim.js --version > "$accept_dir/version.stdout" 2> "$accept_dir/version.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  test ! -s "$accept_dir/version.stderr"
  test "$(wc -l < "$accept_dir/version.stdout")" -eq 1
  rg -q '^agent-session-search-cass-shim .+ \(cass-robot-compat for cm\)$' "$accept_dir/version.stdout"
  awk 'NF == 1 && ($1 + 0) < 2.00 { ok=1 } END { exit !ok }' "$accept_dir/version.time"
Expected: exit 0, one contract line, empty stderr, numeric elapsed strictly <2.00s.

2. General robot search plus copied cm schema and canonical-root proof:
  dist/cli.js sources --json > "$accept_dir/roots.json"
  dist/cass-shim.js search --limit 5 --days 7 --robot -- "vitest" > "$accept_dir/search.json" 2> "$accept_dir/search.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  jq -e '.offset == 0 and .count > 0 and .count == (.hits|length) and (.hits|type) == "array" and all(.hits[]; (.source_path|type) == "string" and (.line_number|type) == "number" and (.agent|type) == "string" and (.snippet|type) == "string" and ((has("created_at")|not) or (.created_at == null) or ((.created_at|type) == "string") or ((.created_at|type) == "number")))' "$accept_dir/search.json"
  node --input-type=module -e 'import {readFileSync,realpathSync} from "node:fs"; import {relative,isAbsolute} from "node:path"; const hits=JSON.parse(readFileSync(process.argv[1],"utf8")).hits; const roots=JSON.parse(readFileSync(process.argv[2],"utf8")).sources.filter((r)=>r.status==="ok").map((r)=>r.root); for (const hit of hits) { const canonical=realpathSync(hit.source_path); if (canonical!==hit.source_path) throw new Error("noncanonical: "+hit.source_path); if (!roots.some((root)=>{const rel=relative(root,canonical); return rel===""||(!rel.startsWith("..")&&!isAbsolute(rel));})) throw new Error("outside resolved roots: "+canonical); }' "$accept_dir/search.json" "$accept_dir/roots.json"
Expected: exit 0; real-cass envelope parses; every hit passes cm's required field types; every source_path equals realpath and is contained by one healthy resolved root. Stderr may contain concise source warnings but no structured total-failure envelope.

3. Known and unknown agent behavior:
  dist/cass-shim.js search --agent claude_code --limit 5 --robot -- "vitest" > "$accept_dir/claude-search.json" 2> "$accept_dir/claude-search.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  jq -e 'all(.hits[]; .agent == "claude_code")' "$accept_dir/claude-search.json"
  dist/cass-shim.js search --agent bogus --robot -- "vitest" > "$accept_dir/bogus-agent.json" 2> "$accept_dir/bogus-agent.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  jq -e '.count == 0 and .hits == [] and .total_matches == 0' "$accept_dir/bogus-agent.json"
  rg -q 'bogus' "$accept_dir/bogus-agent.stderr"
Expected: known-filter hits, if present, all emit claude_code; unknown agent is successful zero-hit output with a diagnostic and no broadened search.

4. Timeline, stats, and real export:
  dist/cass-shim.js timeline --since 7d --json > "$accept_dir/timeline.json" 2> "$accept_dir/timeline.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  jq -e '(.groups|type) == "object" and (.groups|length) > 0 and all(.groups|keys[]; test("^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:00$")) and ([.groups[]?[]? | (.path // .source_path) | select(type=="string" and length>0)] | length) > 0' "$accept_dir/timeline.json"
  dist/cass-shim.js stats --json > "$accept_dir/stats.json" 2> "$accept_dir/stats.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  jq -e '.shim.name == "agent-session-search-cass-shim" and .messages == null and (.conversations|type) == "number"' "$accept_dir/stats.json"
  claude_session=$(jq -r '[.groups[]?[]? | select(.agent == "claude_code") | (.path // .source_path)] | first // empty' "$accept_dir/timeline.json")
  test -n "$claude_session"
  dist/cass-shim.js export --format text -- "$claude_session" > "$accept_dir/export.txt" 2> "$accept_dir/export.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  rg -q '^=== (USER|ASSISTANT|SYSTEM|TOOL) ===$' "$accept_dir/export.txt"
  ! rg -q '^=== UNKNOWN ===$' "$accept_dir/export.txt"
Expected: non-empty UTC-keyed timeline with session paths; shim-marked honest stats; selected recent Claude session exports at least one recognized role and zero UNKNOWN headers. Selecting an unparseable file is not an acceptable sign-off—choose another recent parseable Claude session and record it.

5. Unsupported verb stream/exit discipline:
  set +e
  dist/cass-shim.js bogusverb > "$accept_dir/bogusverb.stdout" 2> "$accept_dir/bogusverb.stderr"
  bogus_status=$?
  set -e
  test "$bogus_status" -eq 2
  test ! -s "$accept_dir/bogusverb.stdout"
  test "$(wc -l < "$accept_dir/bogusverb.stderr")" -ge 1
  jq -e '.error.code == 2 and .error.kind == "usage" and (.error.hint|type) == "string"' "$accept_dir/bogusverb.stderr"
Expected: exit 2, empty stdout, one parseable usage envelope on stderr naming supported behavior. If pretty JSON spans lines, line count is not used as envelope count; jq proves exactly one JSON value.

LIVE CM GATES — EXACT ORDER AND PASS CONDITIONS
Export CASS_PATH only inside this acceptance shell:
  export CASS_PATH="$PWD/dist/cass-shim.js"

1. Doctor:
  cm doctor > "$accept_dir/shim-doctor.stdout" 2> "$accept_dir/shim-doctor.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  rg -q 'agent-session-search-cass-shim' "$accept_dir/shim-doctor.stdout" "$accept_dir/shim-doctor.stderr"
  rg -qi 'no index|searched live|live' "$accept_dir/shim-doctor.stdout" "$accept_dir/shim-doctor.stderr"
  ! rg -qi 'index[_ -]?missing' "$accept_dir/shim-doctor.stdout" "$accept_dir/shim-doctor.stderr"
Expected: cm reports cass available, visibly identifies the shim/live no-index engine, and emits no index-missing diagnosis.

2. Context latency and functional output — first timed context is "cold", next three are "warm":
  : > "$accept_dir/context-times.tsv"
  for label in cold warm1 warm2 warm3; do
    /usr/bin/time -f '%e' -o "$accept_dir/$label.time" cm context "recent work" --json > "$accept_dir/$label.json" 2> "$accept_dir/$label.stderr"
    test "$?" -eq 0 || exit 1
    jq -e . "$accept_dir/$label.json" >/dev/null || exit 1
    printf '%s\t%s\n' "$label" "$(cat "$accept_dir/$label.time")" >> "$accept_dir/context-times.tsv"
  done
  awk -F '\t' 'NF != 2 || ($2 + 0) >= 4.00 { exit 1 } END { if (NR != 4) exit 1 }' "$accept_dir/context-times.tsv"
  jq -e '([.. | objects | select(has("degraded"))] | length) == 0 and ([.. | objects | select((has("source_path") or has("path")) and (has("snippet") or has("content")))] | length) > 0' "$accept_dir/cold.json"
Expected: exactly four numeric measurements labeled cold/warm1/warm2/warm3; each process exits 0 and is strictly <4.00s (8s timeout is automatic failure); every output parses as JSON; cold output has at least one history-like entry and no degraded field anywhere. Do not run an untimed live cm context before this series.

3. Reflect dry-run:
  cm reflect --dry-run --days 1 > "$accept_dir/reflect.stdout" 2> "$accept_dir/reflect.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  rg -qi '[1-9][0-9]*[[:space:]]+session' "$accept_dir/reflect.stdout" "$accept_dir/reflect.stderr"
  ! rg -q '=== UNKNOWN ===' "$accept_dir/reflect.stdout" "$accept_dir/reflect.stderr"
  ! rg -qi 'export[ -]?fallback|falling back' "$accept_dir/reflect.stdout" "$accept_dir/reflect.stderr"
Expected: exit 0; output proves at least one live session is processed in dry-run mode, contains no UNKNOWN role block, reports no export fallback, and writes no reflection state.

CHILD-CLEANUP GATE — BASELINE DIFF, NOT RAW PGREP
  ps -C fff-mcp -o pid= 2>/dev/null | awk '{$1=$1; if ($1 != "") print $1}' | sort -n > "$accept_dir/fff.after"
  comm -13 "$accept_dir/fff.before" "$accept_dir/fff.after" > "$accept_dir/fff.new"
  for attempt in 1 2 3 4 5; do
    test ! -s "$accept_dir/fff.new" && break
    sleep 1
    ps -C fff-mcp -o pid= 2>/dev/null | awk '{$1=$1; if ($1 != "") print $1}' | sort -n > "$accept_dir/fff.after"
    comm -13 "$accept_dir/fff.before" "$accept_dir/fff.after" > "$accept_dir/fff.new"
  done
  test ! -s "$accept_dir/fff.new"
Expected: exit 0 after at most five seconds; no PID absent from the pre-acceptance baseline remains. Pre-existing fff-mcp processes are not misclassified.

ROLLBACK AND CONFIG-INTEGRITY GATE
  unset CASS_PATH
  env -u CASS_PATH cm doctor > "$accept_dir/real-doctor.after.stdout" 2> "$accept_dir/real-doctor.after.stderr"
  test "$?" -eq 0
  ! rg -q 'agent-session-search-cass-shim' "$accept_dir/real-doctor.after.stdout" "$accept_dir/real-doctor.after.stderr"

Expected: real cass doctor is restored with no shim marker; config state remains byte-identical by SHA-256 or remains absent. Environment-only activation performed no config rewrite or migration.

FINAL SIGN-OFF
  npm run check
  npm test
  npm run build
  git diff --check
Expected: all exit 0 after any owner-scoped red-first fix. Review every artifact under accept_dir; sign off only when all earlier expected outcomes are present.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Built search hits pass the copied cm schema and canonical-root proof; agent/timeline/stats/export/usage contracts pass.
- cm doctor identifies shim-marked live/no-index stats without index-missing diagnosis.
- Four context samples (one cold, three warm) are each <4.00s, return real history, and contain no degraded field.

FAILURE POLICY / CANDIDATE FOLLOW-UPS (OUT OF SCOPE)
Any schema-invalid hit, noncanonical/out-of-root path, degraded context, UNKNOWN export, reflect fallback, >=4.00s timing, cm timeout, orphan, missing shim marker, broken rollback, or config change blocks release. Do not persist activation, mask failure with a cache/index/passthrough, relax an assertion, add a new module, or expand shared FFF performance. If shared-backend performance is implicated rather than shim adapter work, record it only as a candidate follow-up (out of scope).

FINAL INDEPENDENT SIGN-OFF GATE
  test -s "$accept_dir/context-times.tsv" && test ! -s "$accept_dir/fff.new" && cmp -s "$accept_dir/config.before" "$accept_dir/config.after" && npm run check && npm test && npm run build

engine}` info the shim's `stats` verb already emits for `cm doctor`), so operators diagnosing this

title: Investigate cross-agent current-session demotion signals
description:
## Outcome
Decide how `search_sessions` should identify and demote the caller's current live session outside Codex without adding unsafe public API surface or transcript parsing.

## Source of truth
- Deferred from: `bd-3tv.2` core recency/density ranking.
- Prototype finding: `docs/prototypes/findings/recency-ranking-prototype-findings.md`.
- Current scoped behavior: Codex current-session demotion may use `CODEX_THREAD_ID` when it matches a candidate `sessionId`.
- Preserve: single `search_sessions` MCP tool, FFF backend, and score-free normal candidate payloads.

## Success criteria
- Inventory plausible current-session identifiers for supported agents: Codex, Claude, Cursor, Pi, Hermes, and Pool.
- Distinguish reliable environment/client signals from unsafe guesses based on transcript text or path coincidence.
- Capture the decision in `docs/prototypes/findings/current-session-demotion-signals.md` or an equivalently named findings doc.
- Recommend one of:
  - implement additional safe signals,
  - keep Codex-only demotion for now,
  - add an explicit design note for why this is not reliable yet.
- If implementation is recommended, create or update implementation Beads with exact signal fields, failure behavior, tests, and non-goals.
- If no implementation is recommended, close with evidence and a no-change rationale.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do investigate and document the cross-agent signal contract.
- Do inspect current local/session-path conventions enough to classify each supported agent.
- Do not implement ranking changes in this investigation bead.
- Do not add a new MCP tool, transcript parser, embedding/index, custom store, or normal response field.
- Do not infer currentness from transcript body content, previews, snippets, or user query text.

## Failure behavior
- Missing or undocumented agent signals lead to documented uncertainty, not guessed demotion.
- If a signal can identify another session incorrectly, prefer no demotion.
- If evidence is inconclusive, leave this bead `needs-info` or create a narrower investigation bead naming the missing fact.
- If local roots or agent installs are absent, record that absence as evidence instead of substituting assumptions.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Ranking contract: `bd-3tv.2`.
- Candidate session id extraction: `src/search.ts` `sessionIdFromPath`.
- Input/context contracts: `src/types.ts`, `src/tool.ts`, `src/cli.ts`.
- Root conventions: `src/roots.ts`, `CONTEXT.md`, and `DESIGN.md`.
- Durable output: `docs/prototypes/findings/current-session-demotion-signals.md`.

## Validation
```bash
br dep cycles --json
bv --robot-insights
bv --robot-plan
npm run check:beads
```
Docs-only investigation can close without `npm test`; implementation follow-ups must define code validation.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Does not block `bd-3tv`; it is deferred scope outside the prototype-backed production lane.
- May run in parallel with `bd-5se` and `bd-3tv.4` if it owns only its findings doc and any follow-up Beads.
- Should not run in parallel with code implementation of `bd-3tv.2` unless the agent only documents signal research.
- Single-owner risk: any future current-session ranking helper in `src/search.ts`; this bead should not edit it.

## Closure evidence
Close with signal inventory, findings doc path, recommendation, linked follow-up Beads if any, validation commands, and rationale for unsupported agents.
close_reason: Completed docs-only investigation in docs/prototypes/findings/current-session-demotion-signals.md. Recommendation: keep current-session demotion Codex-only for now; CODEX_THREAD_ID is the only verified exact current-session signal matching indexed local candidate sessionId. Claude/Pi have transcript ids but no verified current runtime signal; Cursor resume chat ids, Pool history latest, cwd/mtime/content matches, and Claude remote session ids are not safe local demotion signals. Validation: br dep cycles --json passed with 0 cycles; bv --robot-insights and bv --robot-plan ran; npm run check:beads failed because tracked .beads/issues.jsonl has unstaged bead state changes from active claims/closeout, with no commit/stage performed.

title: Add FFF dependency preflight and setup path
description:
Context/background:
agent-session-search shells out to the external fff-mcp binary through the MCP stdio client. fff-mcp is not currently published as an npm package, so npm install cannot be the whole setup story. The repo needs an explicit way for a future agent to detect whether FFF is present and know how to install it.

Scope:
- Add a setup/preflight command or script that checks for fff-mcp on PATH and reports its version or health status when available.
- When fff-mcp is missing, print actionable install guidance using the official FFF MCP installer URL and mention reviewing the script first if desired.
- Avoid silently running remote curl installers during npm install.
- Document any relevant environment variables such as AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH_FFF_DB_DIR and AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH_CONFIG.

Out of scope:
- Vendoring FFF.
- Reimplementing FFF with rg, SQLite, embeddings, or CASS.
acceptance_criteria:
- A documented command verifies fff-mcp availability and fails with a clear message when it is missing.
- The command succeeds on a machine where fff-mcp is installed and shows enough information to debug PATH/version issues.
- Missing FFF guidance points to the official FFF MCP installer and does not imply npm install installs FFF.
- The server/CLI behavior remains unchanged except for clearer setup diagnostics where appropriate.
close_reason: Implemented FFF preflight command, docs, and tests

title: Implement deterministic query rewriter
description:
Context/background:
The PRD calls for natural-language queries to expand into a small set of FFF-friendly literal patterns. For the fastest initial MCP path, this can trail the identity/original-query fallback, but it remains part of the desired V1 completeness.

Scope:
- Implement deterministic query rewriting with no model calls.
- Emit exact phrases, file paths, branch names, PR/issue/bead IDs, commands, test names, error strings, stack trace fragments, symbols, package names, configured synonyms, and obvious snake/camel/kebab variants.
- Drop broad generic terms such as bug, code, problem, and thing.
- Respect maxPatterns and fall back to the original query when rewriting produces nothing.
- Integrate with search_sessions expandedPatterns.

Out of scope:
- Semantic/vector search.
- Model-based rewriting.
- Custom ranking.

Reasoning:
This improves recall, but the initial useful search can ship with original-query matching first. Keep this bead lower priority so it does not block the MCP scaffold and fanout path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Rewriter emits 1 to maxPatterns literal patterns for representative natural-language prompts.
- Exact quoted phrases, paths, commands, error fragments, and IDs are preserved.
- Generic filler terms are not emitted as standalone patterns.
- Synonym config is applied when present.
- Empty/failed rewrites fall back to the original query.
- search_sessions uses the rewriter when available and still reports expandedPatterns.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Unit-test representative queries from the PRD and edge cases.
- Tests should assert output patterns, caps, and fallback behavior, not private parser internals.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic query rewriting with tests

title: Add agent-planned session search probes
description:
Context/background:
agent-session-search is an agent-facing recall tool, not a human search box. Agents often receive conversational user requests plus operational context. The MCP surface should let the calling LLM agent provide planned literal probes and lightweight context instead of forcing the tool to infer everything from one yappy query.

Scope:
- Add optional planned query/probe input to search_sessions while keeping query as the original request.
- Add optional operational context metadata for audit/debug and agent guidance.
- Fan out planned probes through existing deterministic mechanical expansion and preserve original query/pattern provenance.
- Update MCP tool description/README/PRD to instruct agents to provide context/probes when useful.

Acceptance criteria:
- Existing single-query callers keep working.
- Planned probes are searched in addition to or instead of deterministic fallback as documented.
- Output/debug exposes enough provenance for an agent to see what was searched.
- Tests cover the public search_sessions behavior and MCP input parsing.
close_reason: Completed and verified

comments.author: ben
comments.text: Implemented agent-planned queries plus operationalContext through the public search_sessions API/MCP schema; updated PRD/README/tool description. Verified with npm run check, npm test, npm run check:fff, and npm run build.

title: Add empty unscoped evidence resultsShape regression test
description: Add a small public-API regression test for an unscoped resultsDisplayMode: evidence search whose backend returns no hits. Assert resultsShape is evidence_groups, results is an empty array, and warnings remain empty when the backend succeeds. This covers the empty-group case without testing private helpers such as toEvidenceGroups directly.
close_reason: Merged into bd-5se so both unscoped evidence edge cases land as one reviewable public-API regression-test packet: same-path multi-source grouping plus empty evidence_groups shape. No functionality dropped.

title: Implement config and root resolver for session sources
description:
Context/background:
search_sessions must search multiple real source roots while preserving canonical paths and handling missing roots gracefully. Supported built-in sources are codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes. Roots are configurable, but V1 needs sensible defaults for Ben's machine.

Scope:
- Define source names and root config shape for built-in and custom sources.
- Load config from ~/.config/agent-session-search/config.json when present.
- Provide default roots for codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes as described in PRD.md.
- Apply source filtering from search_sessions input, including sources: "all" and custom source names.
- Validate root existence/readability and return searchedSources entries with status ok, missing, or failed plus structured warnings.
- Preserve include patterns in config, including Cursor's */agent-transcripts/* shape.

Out of scope:
- Actually searching files.
- Ranking project-local results.
- Creating AGENTS.md or Matt-skill workflow docs.

Reasoning:
Root resolution is a high-leverage seam. It lets the MCP tool search all configured session stores while tolerating machines that only have some agents installed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Built-in defaults exist for /Users/ben/.codex/sessions, /Users/ben/.claude/projects, /Users/ben/.pi/agent/sessions, /Users/ben/.cursor/projects, and /Users/ben/.hermes/sessions.
- Configured roots can override or extend defaults.
- Missing roots produce warnings and do not fail the whole request.
- Source filtering selects only requested sources.
- Custom source names are accepted at runtime.
- Cursor include patterns are represented so downstream search can avoid irrelevant files.
- Output includes searchedSources with canonical root paths where possible.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Unit-test defaults, config override, source filtering, missing roots, unreadable roots where practical, and custom sources.
- Use temporary directories for behavior tests instead of depending on Ben's real home paths.
close_reason: Implemented config/root resolver with built-in roots, config merge, source filtering, canonical paths, and missing-root warnings. Verified npm test, npm run check, npm run build, and CLI root status smoke.

title: epic: days-workspace filters — --days / --workspace deterministic filters for search_sessions (CLI + MCP parity)
description:
Epic tracking the delivery of deterministic `--days` / `workspace` filters for the session-search engine, with 1:1 parity between the CLI (`--days`, `--workspace`) and the managed MCP `search_sessions` tool.

## Plan of record
docs/plans/2026-07-20-002-feat-days-workspace-filters-plan.md
(council synthesis; artifact_readiness: implementation-ready). Read it plus DESIGN.md, root CONTEXT.md, and CLAUDE.md before starting. The child beads are written to be self-contained; the plan is the authoritative anchor map.

## Why this exists (origin: cm-decoupling)
cass (the indexed session-search tool that cass-memory / `cm` depends on) is structurally broken on this machine (WAL autocheckpoint livelock, partial-index rebuilds, daily_stats OOM). This repo's grep-based engine (FFF-backed) searches the same corpus with no index. To let `cm` consume this engine via the companion cass-compat shim, `search_sessions` needs the two filters cm's search calls send: `days` and `workspace`. This initiative ships those two filters as deterministic drops.

## Load-bearing guardrails (apply to every child)
- Filters are DETERMINISTIC DROPS, never ranking signals (DESIGN.md). No ranking coupling, no token heuristics in the filter path, no recency-ranking changes.
- Managed one-tool boundary preserved: `tools/list` still shows exactly `search_sessions`; additive optional fields only, NO result-contract version bump.
- Native FFF lane untouched: `agent-session-search-native-mcp` and raw FFF tool schemas stay free of `days`/`workspace` (fail-closed policy in src/fff-native-policy.ts not involved).
- No custom index / SQLite search store / embeddings / markdown exports.
- Additive optional fields only — reverting the branch restores prior behavior (filter-less requests, fingerprints, and result shapes untouched).

## Checkpoint structure (dependency-ordered A -> B -> C)
- Checkpoint A (kernel + engine core): split into two beads.
  - A1: pure filter kernel `src/session-filters.ts` + foundational `src/types.ts` fields + test-first unit suite.
  - A2: wire the kernel into `src/search.ts` (pre-cap filtering, backend-cap deferral, warning + metadata echo, 2 of the 6 groupCandidates mirror surfaces) + core engine tests.
- Checkpoint B (surfaces): managed MCP `search_sessions` schema + CLI flags + help/capabilities/robot-docs, the remaining 4 of the 6 groupCandidates mirror surfaces, pinned-fingerprint tests. Never land B without A — surfaces are dead wiring without the core.
- Checkpoint C (docs + verification tail): docs across cli.md/mcp.md/DESIGN.md/CONTEXT.md/UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md/troubleshooting.md + the full verification contract (gates + live-corpus proofs).

## Highest-probability bug (call-out for the whole graph)
The `groupCandidates` replay path has SIX mirror surfaces that must all carry the filter fields consistently, or filtered group-followup replay silently breaks. Two live in src/search.ts (owned by A2: effectiveSearchInput, groupFollowup builder); four live in src/tool.ts (owned by B: strict zod schema, validateGroupCandidatesFollowup mismatch checks, normalizeGroupCandidatesShorthand, correctedShape). Server-prepared payloads must carry the CANONICALIZED workspace so replay does not reinterpret `.` or a symlink under a different cwd. Guarded by the replay round-trip test, the strict-schema mismatch test, and the pinned-fingerprint tests.

## Cross-initiative dependency (coordination note)
A parallel initiative — the cass-compat shim (plan: docs/plans/2026-07-20-002-feat-cass-compat-shim-plan.md) — HARD-DEPENDS on this initiative; it consumes the `days`/`workspace` fields in-process and ships AFTER this one. Another agent is creating the shim's beads concurrently and will add the cross-plan dependency edge FROM THEIR SIDE. Do not create that edge here. This epic carries "days-workspace filters" in its title so the shim beads can find it.

## Definition of done
All four child beads closed with evidence; `npm run check && npm test && npm run build && npm run smoke && npm run check:dcg` green; every live-corpus proof in the plan's verification contract independently checkable; managed `tools/list` still exactly one tool; native FFF schemas free of the new fields.
close_reason: All seven child/checkpoint beads closed; commits through d9f8c13; static gates and all nine live-corpus proofs green; managed one-tool and native isolation invariants verified.

title: feat(session-filters): pure days/workspace filter kernel + types + unit tests (Checkpoint A1)
description:
Checkpoint A1: implement the pure days/workspace session-file filter kernel and the foundational shared types. This bead is intentionally isolated from `src/search.ts`; `bd-okn.2` owns coordinator wiring, caps, warnings, and metadata echo.

## Outcome

1. Update `src/types.ts`:
   - Add `days?: number` and `workspace?: string` to `SearchSessionsInput` and `GroupCandidatesFollowupInput`.
   - Add optional `filters?: { days?: number; workspace?: string }` to `SearchSessionsMetadata`.
   - Add `"filters_removed_all_results"` to the warning-code union.
2. Add `src/session-filters.ts`, a candidate/FFF/ranking/warning-agnostic module exporting:
   - `encodeWorkspaceDirName(workspace)`, exactly `workspace.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9]/g, "-")`. Encoding is lossy: never decode a recorded directory name.
   - `stripDashes`, used to trim only leading/trailing dashes before equality comparison.
   - `workspaceEncodedSegmentMatch`, which compares a leading-dash-guarded recorded path segment against the encoded workspace by exact equality after dash trimming. It must accept Claude's `-data-projects-...` form and both OMP `--...--` and bare forms, but never prefix-match.
   - `canonicalWorkspacePath`, which expands `~`, resolves relative input to an absolute normalized path, and attempts `realpath`. Preserve both the absolute-resolved and realpath forms when they differ; for a missing path, retain the normalized absolute form.
   - `prepareSessionFileFilters`, which computes `cutoff = now() - days * 86_400_000` once and precomputes canonical and encoded workspace forms. `mtime >= cutoff` is inclusive. `now` is injected; predicates must not read the wall clock independently.
   - Memoized async `resultPassesSessionFileFilters` / `applySessionFileFilters`, keyed by `{source, path}`, not by path alone.
3. Accept injected callbacks for mtime lookup and metadata project-path lookup. The evaluator must expose enough drop-reason information for `bd-okn.2` to aggregate exactly `days`, `workspace`, and `stat_failed`, without coupling this module to search result/warning types.

## Predicate contract and evaluation order

- Compose supplied fields with AND semantics.
- Evaluate, cheap to expensive: direct containment against either canonical workspace form; exact encoded-segment match against either encoded form; mtime cutoff; metadata project-path containment against either canonical form.
- Metadata lookup is a last resort only after the direct containment and encoded workspace lanes fail. With `days` alone, do not invoke metadata lookup.
- An unstatable path is dropped as `stat_failed` only when `days` is active; with no days filter it is retained unless the workspace predicate independently rejects it.
- Workspace containment is directional: a candidate/metadata project path equal to or below the requested workspace matches; its parent, sibling, or a path containing only a similar token does not.
- Memoize all I/O verdict work per `{source, path}`. Repeated evaluation of that key invokes each applicable callback at most once. The same path under two sources must not share a metadata-sensitive verdict.

## Explicit boundaries

- Do not wire `src/search.ts`, defer caps, emit warnings, or construct response metadata here; those belong to `bd-okn.2`.
- Do not add knowledge of FFF hits, candidates, match groups, ranking, or warning objects.
- Do not add another transcript parser. Metadata project paths arrive only through the injected callback so the coordinator can reuse its bounded early-record reader.
- Do not modify `src/followup.ts`; verify only that its existing `stableJson` behavior skips undefined values. Do not touch `src/roots.ts`, `src/fff-backend.ts`, or query rewriting.
- Native FFF behavior, ranking, recency scoring, indexes, embeddings, SQLite, and multiple-workspace support remain out of scope.

## Mandatory TDD execution order

Use `test/session-filters.test.ts` as the public filter-kernel seam. Work in vertical slices: for each numbered scenario below, add one behavior test first, run `npx vitest run test/session-filters.test.ts`, and confirm it fails for the intended missing/incorrect behavior (RED); add only enough production code to make that scenario pass (GREEN); then refactor names/duplication without changing behavior and rerun the file before starting the next scenario. Do not write the whole suite or implementation in one horizontal batch.

1. Cutoff math: boundary `-1ms` drops, exact boundary passes, `+1ms` and future mtime pass; injected `now` is sampled once.
2. Stat failure: unstatable drops with `stat_failed` when days is active and is retained when days is absent.
3. Exact encoded segments: Claude form and both OMP forms match.
4. Collision guards: `-...-themodernsocial` does not match `-...-themodernsocial-agent-platform`; reject prefix, sibling, and missing-leading-dash false positives.
5. Direct containment and dual-form symlink matching: exercise distinct absolute-resolved and realpath forms.
6. Metadata fallback: descendant/equal matches; parent, sibling, and token-only paths do not.
7. AND composition: both days and workspace must pass; preserve the correct drop reason.
8. Memoization and lane order: applicable stat/metadata callbacks run at most once per `{source,path}`; metadata is skipped when an earlier workspace lane succeeds and for days-only requests; identical paths under two sources keep separate metadata verdicts.

Expected RED evidence must be an assertion failure or missing-export/module failure caused by the scenario under development, not an environment/configuration failure. Preserve the final tests as behavior specifications through exported seams; do not assert private helper call order except the explicitly required injected-I/O counts and lane-skipping contract.

## Failure behavior

- Missing/nonexistent workspace input canonicalizes deterministically and yields no matches; it is not a kernel exception.
- Files not provably within the days window because stat fails are excluded with `stat_failed`.
- I/O failures are predicate outcomes, not whole-search failures.

## Verification gates (all independently checkable)

1. `npx vitest run test/session-filters.test.ts` exits 0 and reports `test/session-filters.test.ts` green with zero failed tests; the named cases cover all eight ordered scenario groups above.
2. `npm run check` exits 0 with no TypeScript diagnostics, proving the new exports and `src/types.ts` additions typecheck.
3. `npm test` exits 0 with zero failed Vitest files/tests, proving the foundational optional fields and new module do not regress filter-less behavior.
4. `git diff --exit-code -- src/followup.ts src/roots.ts src/fff-backend.ts src/query-rewriter.ts` exits 0 and prints no output; implementation changes are confined to `src/types.ts`, new `src/session-filters.ts`, and `test/session-filters.test.ts`.
close_reason: Implemented pure days/workspace filter kernel and shared types; test/session-filters.test.ts 8/8 green, npm run check green, full Vitest 20 files/273 tests green, protected-file diff gate clean; commit 28ff12b.

title: epic(search): coordinate Checkpoint A2 filter engine wiring
description:
Checkpoint A2 coordinating container. The former single bead mixed three independently testable responsibilities in src/search.ts. Its existing scope is now partitioned, without additions or omissions, into three ordered children:

1. bd-okn.2.1 — integrate the prepared filter kernel into source slots, defer backend caps, restore wrapper caps, reuse bounded metadata I/O, and preserve ranking/partial-source behavior.
2. bd-okn.2.2 — aggregate days/workspace/stat_failed removals, emit the narrowly gated filters_removed_all_results warning, and echo canonical metadata.filters.
3. bd-okn.2.3 — carry canonical days/workspace through the two coordinator-owned groupCandidates replay mirrors and keep more.evidence filter-free.

Execution order is bd-okn.1 -> bd-okn.2.1 -> bd-okn.2.2 -> bd-okn.2.3 -> close this container -> bd-okn.3. The existing bd-okn.3 dependency on this container preserves the original Checkpoint A before Checkpoint B ordering.

## Scope partition and boundaries

The union of the three children is exactly the prior bd-okn.2 scope: preparation/now, slot wiring, pre-cap/backend-cap correctness, metadata callback reuse, reason accounting, warning truth table, metadata echo, ranking/partial-source invariants, and the two src/search.ts replay surfaces. Detailed acceptance criteria live only in the children to prevent duplicate implementation.

No child may change src/tool.ts, src/cli.ts, src/help.ts, documentation, src/followup.ts, src/roots.ts, src/fff-backend.ts, query rewriting, native FFF behavior, ranking formulas, or more.evidence inheritance. The later surface bead owns the other four groupCandidates mirrors. Candidate follow-ups outside these boundaries are out of scope and must be recorded only as candidate follow-up notes.

## TDD coordination contract

This container carries no production edit and therefore has no standalone RED/GREEN implementation loop. Its verification substitute is the completed child sequence. Each child must execute its named test first and observe an intended failing assertion before production changes, then GREEN the smallest vertical slice and refactor while targeted tests stay green. The cap-starvation characterization in bd-okn.2.1 is the first RED across all A2 implementation.

Do not close this container merely because descriptions are complete. Close it only after all three children are closed and the aggregate gates below pass.

## Verification gates

1. br show bd-okn.2.1 bd-okn.2.2 bd-okn.2.3 --json | jq -e 'length == 3 and all(.[]; .status == "closed")' exits 0 and prints true.
2. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts exits 0 with test/search.test.ts green and zero failed tests, including the exact cap-starvation, warning, and replay tests named in the children.
3. npm run check exits 0 with no TypeScript diagnostics.
4. npm test exits 0 with zero failed Vitest files/tests, proving filter-less, ranking, source-warning, and continuation regressions remain absent.
5. br dep cycles exits 0 and reports no dependency cycles.
6. git diff --exit-code -- src/tool.ts src/cli.ts src/help.ts src/followup.ts src/roots.ts src/fff-backend.ts exits 0 with no output for the complete A2 checkpoint.
close_reason: Verified children bd-okn.2.1/.2.2/.2.3 closed; search suite 64/64, npm run check green, full Vitest 22 files/301 tests green, no dependency cycles, protected boundaries clean; commit 43b9a61.

title: A2.1: integrate filter kernel before caps and preserve eligible hits
description:
Checkpoint A2.1 partitions the engine-core integration portion of bd-okn.2. It consumes the pure kernel from bd-okn.1, prepares one filter state per search invocation, defers backend caps whenever a filter is active, applies the filter after canonicalization and existing source eligibility but before the wrapper cap, and reuses the bounded metadata reader. Warning aggregation, metadata.filters response echo, and groupCandidates replay carry belong to sibling children and are explicitly excluded here.

## Outcome

1. In src/search.ts, prepare session-file filters once in CoordinatedSessionSearch.searchSessions after group replay normalization.
   - Add now?: () => number to CreateSessionSearchOptions as the test-only time seam.
   - Sample now once per searchSessions invocation and pass one prepared cutoff/state to every source slot.
   - Canonicalize the effective workspace once; retain both resolved and realpath forms only inside the prepared matcher and make the single canonical value available to later response/replay work.
2. Extend shouldDeferBackendCap so active days or workspace filters are restrictive, with the same uncapped-backend/capped-wrapper policy as exact paths or restrictive include.
   - Evidence requests with an explicit cap must send maxResults: undefined to the backend whenever a new filter is active.
   - Candidate/debug behavior remains capped only at the existing wrapper/grouping layer.
3. Thread the prepared state through SourceSearchSlotInput.
   - Preserve the existing synchronous resultMatchesSourceFilters pass.
   - Apply the memoized async session-file filter only after canonicalizeSearchResult and existing include/path eligibility, and before maybeCapResults.
   - Preserve survivor order; do not couple filtering to ranking or debug score components.
4. Reuse projectSignalsFromCandidateMetadata by loosening its parameter only to {source, path}; pass its existing bounded early-record project paths into the kernel callback.
   - No second parser, new metadata format, new byte/line budget, or shared ranking stat cache.
5. Preserve existing partial-source behavior: a failed source still warns while filtered survivors from another source remain usable.

## Load-bearing cap-starvation contract

Filtering only before the wrapper slice is insufficient because an FFF-side cap may discard every later eligible hit. With cap 1 and an ineligible first backend hit followed by an eligible second hit, the backend call must be uncapped, the second hit must survive filtering, and the wrapper must still return exactly one hit. This contract applies to evidence and candidate/debug paths; it is not a ranking change.

## Failure and boundary contract

- An unstatable path is dropped only when days is active, using the kernel stat_failed verdict; without days the filter wiring does not create a new failure.
- Metadata lookup failure/absence is a workspace non-match, not a whole-source exception.
- Missing/unreadable roots and backend failures retain existing partial-warning behavior.
- Do not aggregate reason counts, emit filters_removed_all_results, or add metadata.filters in this child; bd-okn.2.2 owns those response-observability changes.
- Do not add days/workspace to groupFollowup or the follow-up branch of effectiveSearchInput; bd-okn.2.3 owns the two coordinator replay mirrors.
- Do not change src/tool.ts, src/cli.ts, src/help.ts, docs, src/followup.ts, src/roots.ts, src/fff-backend.ts, query rewriting, native FFF schemas, more.evidence, or ranking formulas/components.

## Mandatory TDD sequence

The pre-agreed public seams are createSessionSearch/createBackend in test/search.test.ts plus observable SearchSessionsOutput. Use temporary session files and utimes for mtime behavior. For every slice: add the named behavior test first; run its narrow npx vitest command and confirm a behavior assertion fails for the intended missing wiring (RED); implement only that slice (GREEN); refactor duplication/names while the targeted test remains green; then proceed. Do not batch the suite before implementation.

1. RED FIRST: add a test named exactly "defers backend cap before active filters so a later eligible hit survives". Configure explicit cap 1, an ineligible first backend hit, and an eligible later hit. Final assertions prove backend maxResults is exactly undefined, the eligible later path is returned, and exactly one result survives the wrapper cap. Keep this characterization test permanently.
2. Days behavior: in evidence and candidates modes a 90-day-old tmp file drops at days: 30; the same hit remains without days; an unstatable path drops only with days.
3. Cutoff lifecycle: a multi-source request with injected now calls it exactly once and applies the same inclusive cutoff to all slots.
4. Workspace behavior: direct containment; exact Claude and both OMP encoded segments; sibling -...-extra rejection; codex-named metadata fallback using a first bounded record with {"cwd":"..."}.
5. Composition and neutrality: combined days/workspace requires both; survivor ranking order is unchanged; candidate debug ranking components contain no filter fields.
6. Partial success: one backend/source failure coexists with filtered survivors and the existing source warning.

Backend argument inspection is permitted only for the cap-deferral contract. Other tests observe public search output and do not assert private coordinator call order. Each RED must be a relevant assertion or missing-wiring failure, never fixture/environment/configuration failure.

## Verification gates

1. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts -t "defers backend cap before active filters so a later eligible hit survives" exits 0; exactly that named test is green and its assertions require maxResults === undefined, the later eligible path, and one returned hit.
2. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts -t "days|workspace|cutoff|partial" exits 0 with zero failed selected tests covering both result modes, all workspace lanes, one-sample time, ranking neutrality, and partial success.
3. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts exits 0 with test/search.test.ts green and zero failed tests.
4. npm run check exits 0 with no TypeScript diagnostics for now, prepared-filter threading, and the loosened metadata seam.
5. npm test exits 0 with zero failed Vitest files/tests; filter-less search, ranking, and source-warning behavior remain green.
6. git diff --exit-code -- src/tool.ts src/cli.ts src/help.ts src/followup.ts src/roots.ts src/fff-backend.ts exits 0 with no output; only src/search.ts and test/search.test.ts are changed by this child beyond consuming bd-okn.1.
close_reason: Integrated prepared filters before backend/wrapper caps with bounded metadata reuse; exact cap-starvation gate green, selected filter tests green, search suite 58/58, npm run check green, full Vitest 21 files/290 tests green, protected-file diff gate clean; commit 50c64a5.

title: A2.2: count filter removals and expose warning/metadata
description:
Checkpoint A2.2 partitions the response-observability portion of bd-okn.2. It builds on bd-okn.2.1 filtering and adds only reason-split removal accounting, the narrowly gated filters_removed_all_results warning, and metadata.filters echo. It does not change filter predicates, cap behavior, ranking, or continuation payloads.

## Outcome

1. Add filterRemovedCount to SourceSearchSlotResult with exactly three counters: days, workspace, and stat_failed.
   - Record only removals produced by the new session-file kernel after canonicalization and existing include/path eligibility.
   - Aggregate the counters across all attempted source slots.
   - Lexical misses, skipped/unreadable roots, backend/source failures, and existing include/path-only removals contribute zero.
2. Emit exactly one filters_removed_all_results warning only when all three conditions hold:
   - at least one of days/workspace is active;
   - final returned survivors are zero;
   - aggregate new-filter removals are greater than zero.
3. Make recommendedAction deterministic and reason-aware using only the observed counters:
   - days removal directs the caller to widen days;
   - workspace removal directs the caller to verify/widen the workspace;
   - stat_failed directs the caller to verify session-file readability/mtime availability;
   - mixed reasons may combine those existing remedies, but add no new warning code or response field.
4. Extend searchMetadata so metadata.filters = {days?, workspace?} appears only when at least one filter was supplied.
   - Echo the one canonical workspace produced by bd-okn.2.1.
   - Echo the requested days integer unchanged.
   - Never expose cutoff, now, response time, removal counters, or another nondeterministic value.
5. A syntactically valid nonexistent workspace remains an engine success: when actual backend hits are removed, return results: [], exactly one filter warning, canonical metadata.filters, and no thrown input error.

## Truth table and boundaries

- Filter active + removed > 0 + zero survivors: one filter warning.
- Filter active + removed > 0 + any survivor: no filter warning.
- Filter active + lexical zero hits: no filter warning.
- Source/backend failure without a predicate removal: no filter warning beyond existing source warnings.
- Existing include/path eligibility removes every hit before the kernel: no filter warning.
- Filter inactive: metadata.filters absent and no filter warning.
- Do not alter the kernel in src/session-filters.ts, cap deferral, filtering order, candidate ordering, ranking/debug components, existing warning codes, or source-failure policy.
- Do not add days/workspace to groupCandidates payloads in this child; bd-okn.2.3 owns replay.
- Do not change src/tool.ts, src/cli.ts, src/help.ts, docs, src/followup.ts, src/roots.ts, src/fff-backend.ts, query rewriting, native FFF, or more.evidence.

## Mandatory TDD sequence

The pre-agreed seam is observable SearchSessionsOutput from createSessionSearch in test/search.test.ts. For each slice, write the named behavior test first and run the narrowest npx vitest command; confirm it fails on the missing counter/warning/metadata behavior (RED); implement only enough src/search.ts code to pass (GREEN); refactor without changing output and rerun the target before the next slice.

1. RED FIRST: add a test named exactly "warns once when active filters remove every eligible result". Assert results is empty, exactly one warning has code filters_removed_all_results, and metadata.filters echoes the canonical workspace and/or requested days.
2. Add reason-specific tests for days, workspace, and stat_failed. Each must independently prove the matching recommendedAction remedy and must not rely on internal counter inspection.
3. Add negative-control tests for lexical no-hit, source failure, include-only removal, a partial filtered result set with at least one survivor, and a filter-less request. Each asserts filters_removed_all_results is absent; filter-less output also asserts metadata.filters is absent.
4. Add the nonexistent-workspace case: canonical echo, actual removals, one warning, empty results, and resolved promise rather than an input exception.
5. Add mixed-source aggregation: predicate removals in one slot plus a survivor in another produces no empty-result warning; predicate removals in multiple slots with no survivor still produces exactly one warning.

Tests observe public result/warning/metadata behavior. Do not assert internal helper names or counter storage. Every RED must be the intended behavior assertion failure, not a fixture or environment failure.

## Verification gates

1. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts -t "warns once when active filters remove every eligible result" exits 0 with that exact test green; it proves zero survivors, one warning, and canonical metadata.filters.
2. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts -t "filters_removed_all_results|filter metadata|nonexistent workspace" exits 0 with zero selected failures and covers all three removal remedies plus every negative control.
3. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts exits 0 with test/search.test.ts green and zero failed tests.
4. npm run check exits 0 with no TypeScript diagnostics for slot counters, aggregation, warning construction, and SearchSessionsMetadata.
5. npm test exits 0 with zero failed Vitest files/tests; existing warning/source-partial/filter-less response contracts remain green.
6. git diff --exit-code -- src/session-filters.ts src/tool.ts src/cli.ts src/help.ts src/followup.ts src/roots.ts src/fff-backend.ts exits 0 with no output; this child changes only src/search.ts and test/search.test.ts.
close_reason: Added reason-split removal aggregation, one narrowly gated filter-empty warning, and canonical metadata.filters echo; exact warning gate green, selected observability tests green, search suite 63/63, npm run check green, full Vitest 21 files/295 tests green, protected-file diff clean; commit 857f860.

title: A2.3: carry canonical filters through group candidate replay
description:
Checkpoint A2.3 partitions the coordinator-owned groupCandidates continuation portion of bd-okn.2. It carries canonical days/workspace values across the two src/search.ts replay surfaces and proves the replay remains fingerprint-valid and filtered. The strict zod schema, mismatch validation, shorthand normalization, and corrected-shape mirrors remain in the later MCP/CLI surface bead.

## Outcome

1. Extend the groupCandidates branch of effectiveSearchInput to copy days and workspace from the validated follow-up payload.
   - Replay preparation must see the carried values, so the same filter kernel runs before caps on every page.
   - Canonicalize workspace once per invocation as in bd-okn.2.1; a server-prepared canonical value must not be reinterpreted as the original relative path or symlink spelling.
2. Extend groupFollowup, the coordinator payload/fingerprint builder, to include supplied days/workspace.
   - Server-prepared payloads contain the canonical workspace chosen during the initial search and the original days integer.
   - Filter-less payloads omit both keys, relying on existing stableJson undefined omission so their pinned fingerprint remains byte-identical.
3. Prove a later group page returns only candidates satisfying the carried filters and validates the generated fingerprint.
4. Keep more.evidence filter-free. Focused evidence continues to pin canonical paths and intentionally does not inherit the rolling days/workspace filter.

## Six-surface boundary

This child owns only two src/search.ts mirrors: effectiveSearchInput and groupFollowup. It must compile against the shared GroupCandidatesFollowupInput fields from bd-okn.1. The remaining four mirrors are not duplicated here:
- strict groupCandidatesFollowupSchema;
- validateGroupCandidatesFollowup mismatch checks;
- normalizeGroupCandidatesShorthand;
- correctedShape.

Those four belong to the later surface bead. Do not modify src/followup.ts; verify its current stableJson omission behavior through the public fingerprint result.

## Explicit boundaries

- Do not change filter predicate semantics, cap deferral, removal counters, warning behavior, metadata.filters, ranking, or source-failure behavior.
- Do not add filters to more.evidence or expose cutoff/now.
- Do not change src/tool.ts, src/cli.ts, src/help.ts, docs, src/followup.ts, src/roots.ts, src/fff-backend.ts, query rewriting, or native FFF schemas.
- Do not add new continuation fields, payload versions, fingerprint algorithms, or result-contract versions.

## Mandatory TDD sequence

The pre-agreed seam is the public groupCandidates payload and replay behavior returned by createSessionSearch in test/search.test.ts. For each slice, first add the named behavior test and run the narrowest npx vitest command; confirm the missing carry/replay assertion fails (RED); add only the two coordinator mirror changes needed for GREEN; refactor without output changes and rerun before the next slice.

1. RED FIRST: add a test named exactly "carries canonical session filters through group candidate replay". Initial search uses days plus a relative or symlink workspace and produces a paged candidate group. Assert the server-prepared more.groupCandidates payload contains days and the canonical workspace, and its fingerprint validates on replay.
2. Replay the payload and assert every returned candidate satisfies both carried filters, including a fixture that would appear if either field were lost.
3. Assert the replayed response prepares its next page with the same canonical values rather than the process cwd, relative spelling, or symlink alias.
4. Add the filter-less regression: both optional keys remain absent and the existing filter-less continuation still validates and replays unchanged. Pinned fingerprint literals remain exclusively in the later tool bead.
5. Assert every candidate more.evidence payload omits days/workspace even when the parent candidate search is filtered.

Tests must use server-produced continuation payloads and observable replay results, not private fingerprint helpers or hand-built state except the already established public validation seam. Every RED must fail for missing carry or incorrect payload behavior, not because the later strict tool schema has not yet been changed.

## Verification gates

1. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts -t "carries canonical session filters through group candidate replay" exits 0 with the exact test green; assertions prove canonical days/workspace carry, valid fingerprint, and filtered replay.
2. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts -t "group candidate replay|groupCandidates|more.evidence" exits 0 with zero selected failures; canonical next-page carry, unchanged filter-less replay, and filter-free evidence payloads are covered.
3. npx vitest run test/search.test.ts exits 0 with test/search.test.ts green and zero failed tests.
4. npm run check exits 0 with no TypeScript diagnostics for both coordinator replay mirrors.
5. npm test exits 0 with zero failed Vitest files/tests; existing continuation/fingerprint behavior remains green.
6. git diff --exit-code -- src/tool.ts src/cli.ts src/help.ts src/followup.ts src/roots.ts src/fff-backend.ts exits 0 with no output; this child changes only src/search.ts and test/search.test.ts.
close_reason: Carried canonical days/workspace through coordinator follow-up and replay mirrors while keeping more.evidence/filter-less payloads unchanged; exact replay gate green, search suite 64/64, npm run check green, full Vitest 22 files/301 tests green, protected-file diff clean; commit a0a31c9.

title: feat(surfaces): expose days/workspace on MCP search_sessions + CLI with 1:1 parity (Checkpoint B)
description:
Checkpoint B of the days/workspace filter plan. Expose the already-implemented Checkpoint A filter core through the managed MCP `search_sessions` tool and CLI with 1:1 field names and behavior; complete exactly the four tool-boundary `groupCandidates` mirror surfaces owned here; update agent-facing help/capabilities strings. This bead depends on `bd-okn.2`: do not land dead surface wiring without the core.

## Atomicity and split decision (second polishing pass)
Do **not** split MCP-side and CLI-side work into separate child beads. The authoritative plan defines all of Checkpoint B as one commit-sized parity checkpoint, and the schema/replay mirrors, CLI mapping, discovery text, and exact-object tests must become green together. Either ordering would create an intermediate commit where `days`/`workspace` are exposed on only one public surface, violating the 1:1 parity invariant and inviting fingerprint/help drift. Keep this bead as the single atomic red → green → review unit.

## Outcome and invariants
- MCP fields are `days?: positive integer` and `workspace?: non-empty string`; CLI flags are exactly `--days` and `--workspace`.
- The managed server still advertises exactly one tool, `search_sessions`; optional fields are additive and do not bump the result-contract version.
- Filtered group replay remains copy-exact and fingerprint-checked. The other two of six replay mirrors (`effectiveSearchInput` and the `groupFollowup` builder) belong to `bd-okn.2`; do not duplicate or move them here.
- Native FFF schemas remain unchanged and contain neither field.
- A nonexistent workspace remains valid search input and is handled by the core as empty results + warning + exit 0. Only CLI syntax/value failures described below exit 1.

## Owned implementation surfaces

### `src/tool.ts` — exactly four remaining groupCandidates mirrors
1. Strict schemas: add `days: z.number().int().positive().optional().describe(...)` and `workspace: z.string().min(1).optional().describe(...)` to both `searchSessionsInputSchema` and strict `groupCandidatesFollowupSchema`.
2. `validateGroupCandidatesFollowup`: reject top-level `days` or `workspace` that disagrees with the server-prepared payload, modeled on `maxResultsPerSource`; report the precise `invalidField`.
3. `normalizeGroupCandidatesShorthand`: copy both fields into the normalized structured payload when present.
4. `SearchSessionsInputError.correctedShape`: teach callers that both values must be copied from the prepared payload when present.

All four must agree. Do not modify `src/followup.ts`: its `stableJson` already omits undefined keys, which is why filter-less fingerprints remain byte-stable.

### `src/cli.ts`
- Extend `ParsedArgs`, `KNOWN_OPTIONS`, parsing, and `searchInputFromParsedArgs` for both flags.
- Parse `--days` via `parsePositiveInteger`; parse `--workspace` with the existing required-string/`--cwd` pattern.
- Teach typo correction `--dsys` → `--days` and `--workspce` → `--workspace`.
- `groupCandidatesMixedFlags` must reject either flag mixed with `--group-candidates` and name the offending flag.
- `--days 0`, negative, fractional/non-numeric, or missing value and a missing `--workspace` value are user-input errors: exit 1; JSON mode leaves stdout empty and emits `error.code: "user_input_error"` plus a copy-pasteable `suggestedCommand`.
- Update the existing exact-object mapping assertions in `test/cli.test.ts` together with the production mapping so added optional keys do not cause unrelated failures.

### `src/help.ts`
Update only the surfaces named by the plan:
- `cliHelpText`: usage, two option lines, one filtered example.
- `cliCapabilities`: search usage strings and `contract.warnings.filters_removed_all_results`.
- `robotDocsGuide`: filters are deterministic drops and survive group replay.
- `robotTriage`: one recommended filtered command.
- `mcpSearchSessionsDescription`: one sentence covering the two optional filters.
Preserve pinned wording already asserted by `test/mcp-smoke.test.ts`.

## Mandatory TDD execution order
The plan pre-agrees these public seams: schema/parser + `runSearchSessionsTool` in `test/tool.test.ts`; `parseArgs`/`searchInputFromParsedArgs` and spawned CLI behavior in `test/cli.test.ts`; live stdio tool introspection in `test/mcp-smoke.test.ts`. Test observable contracts at those seams; do not mock internal modules. Work one vertical slice at a time: add one test, run it and observe the intended RED, make the minimum production change for GREEN, then continue. Refactor only after all slices are green, without changing behavior.

1. RED first in `test/tool.test.ts`: valid top-level and structured replay payloads accept `days`/`workspace`; `days: 1.5`, `0`, negative, and `workspace: ""` reject. Implement only the two schemas; rerun to GREEN.
2. RED first in `test/tool.test.ts`: mismatched top-level `days` and `workspace` each throw `SearchSessionsInputError` with the exact `invalidField`, and the teaching `correctedShape` is filter-aware. Implement only validation + corrected shape; rerun to GREEN.
3. RED first in `test/tool.test.ts`: top-level shorthand carries both fields into `groupCandidates`. Implement only shorthand normalization; rerun to GREEN.
4. RED first in `test/tool.test.ts`: pin the filter-less literal `gcf1:52e25b6aeccbeaff`; add `days` and prove the fingerprint differs; prove editing a prepared filter or its fingerprint is rejected. The old-literal assertion is the green control, but the new filtered carry/tamper assertion must be observed failing before surface changes. Do not replace the literal with a value derived by the implementation.
5. RED first in `test/cli.test.ts`: parse/map valid `--days 7 --workspace /data/projects/agent-session-search`, including all existing exact-`toEqual` cases. Implement the argument fields, options, parsers, and mapping; rerun to GREEN.
6. RED first in `test/cli.test.ts`: cover zero/negative/fractional/non-numeric/missing `--days`, missing `--workspace`, both typo suggestions with exact `suggestedCommand`, and each forbidden `--group-candidates` mix naming the offending flag. Implement minimal error/mix behavior; rerun to GREEN.
7. RED first in `test/cli.test.ts` and `test/mcp-smoke.test.ts`: help, capabilities, robot docs/triage, and MCP introspection expose both fields while `tools/list` remains exactly `[{name: "search_sessions"}]`. Implement `src/help.ts` strings and schema descriptions; rerun to GREEN.
8. Refactor only after steps 1–7 are green; rerun every gate below.

## Acceptance criteria
- All four owned replay mirrors carry identical canonical filter values, mismatches teach the exact field, shorthand round-trips, and tampering fails.
- The filter-less pinned fingerprint remains exactly `gcf1:52e25b6aeccbeaff`; a filtered payload does not share it.
- CLI and MCP accept the same valid values and reject the same invalid numeric/string shapes at their respective input boundaries.
- CLI JSON parse failures exit 1 with empty stdout and the documented error envelope/suggestion; a nonexistent workspace reaches search and is not treated as a parse failure.
- Help/capabilities/robot surfaces mention both filters; managed introspection shows both optional fields and exactly one managed tool; native schemas remain free of them.

## Non-goals
No filter predicate, cutoff, ranking, warning-generation, metadata-echo, or backend-cap changes (Checkpoint A); no prose docs (Checkpoint C); no native-lane schema changes; no contract-version bump; no changes to `src/followup.ts`.

## Independently checkable verification gates
Run from `/data/projects/agent-session-search` in this order:
1. `npm run check` exits 0 with no TypeScript diagnostics.
2. `npm test -- test/tool.test.ts` exits 0; output reports that file green, including the exact old fingerprint literal, changed filtered fingerprint, four mirror-surface cases, and tamper rejection.
3. `npm test -- test/cli.test.ts` exits 0; output reports that file green, including valid mapping, all value/missing-value failures, both typo suggestions, both group-mix failures, and help/capabilities/triage assertions.
4. `npm test -- test/mcp-smoke.test.ts` exits 0; introspection assertions find optional `days`/`workspace` and `tools.map(name)` equals only `["search_sessions"]`.
5. `npm test -- test/native-mcp-smoke.test.ts` exits 0 (the live FFF case may skip only when its existing availability guard applies); no native schema fixture or expected schema gains either filter.
6. `npm test` exits 0 with zero failed suites/tests, proving exact-object regressions elsewhere were updated.
7. `npm run smoke` exits 0 and the managed MCP stdio smoke suite remains green.
