title: docs+verify: document days/workspace filters + run full verification contract (Checkpoint C)
description:
Checkpoint C, the documentation and definition-of-done tail for the shipped `days`/`workspace` filters. This bead depends on `bd-okn.3`. It changes documentation and docs-contract assertions only, then executes the plan's verification contract; it must not repair product behavior or broaden the feature.

## Documentation contract
Document only the behavior already specified and implemented:
- `docs/cli.md`: add `--days`/`--workspace` to the flag table and explain the rolling mtime window, inclusive cutoff, unstatable-file drop when days is active, workspace containment/exact dash-segment/metadata lanes, lossy dash encoding, and the residual punctuation-collision limitation.
- `docs/mcp.md`: add `days`/`workspace` to the input table; explain AND composition, canonical workspace echo, replay carry-through, rolling replay cutoff, and `filters_removed_all_results`; tell MCP callers and the companion shim to send an absolute workspace because relative input resolves against server cwd.
- `DESIGN.md`: add both optional fields to the inlined `SearchSessionsInput` and state that session filters are deterministic drops, never ranking signals.
- `CONTEXT.md`: add `src/session-filters.ts` to Key Modules and the deterministic-drop guardrail.
- `UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md`: define `Session Filter`, `Days Filter`, and `Workspace Filter`; keep all three distinct from ranking-side `Project Match`.
- `docs/troubleshooting.md`: explain recovery for `filters_removed_all_results`, including checking the canonical workspace and widening/removing filters.
- `test/readme.test.ts`: pin the new terms, input fields, deterministic-drop distinction, and warning text in the corresponding docs.

Do not add configuration defaults, multiple workspaces, snapshot cutoffs, evidence-payload inheritance, native-lane fields, ranking changes, or any other behavior excluded by the plan.

## Test/verification-first execution order
This is primarily docs + verification. The only implementation-bearing test cycle is the docs contract; all remaining “tests” are the exact acceptance probes below.

1. Before editing docs, add the planned assertions to `test/readme.test.ts`. Run `npm test -- test/readme.test.ts` and observe RED because the new terms/warning/fields are absent.
2. Update one documentation surface at a time with only the text listed above, rerunning `npm test -- test/readme.test.ts` after each vertical slice until GREEN. Expected values must be fixed plan vocabulary, not strings generated from the docs under test.
3. Refactor wording only after the docs-contract test is green; rerun it to prove no contract text was lost.
4. Execute every static and live verification gate below. These commands are the verification substitute for production-code TDD in this docs-only checkpoint.
5. If a live probe exposes a product defect, stop: this bead is not done. Reopen the owning A1/A2/B bead and fix/test there; do not change source behavior or paper over the gap in documentation from this bead.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every listed document explains the same shipped contract and uses the ubiquitous-language distinction between deterministic filtering and ranking.
- `test/readme.test.ts` fails before the relevant docs exist and passes after them.
- All static gates and every live-corpus proof below satisfy their explicit expected outcome.
- Managed `tools/list` remains exactly `search_sessions`; native FFF schemas contain neither field.
- Missing roots and nonexistent workspaces remain successful searches with warnings, never hard failures.

## Failure behavior and boundaries
If any gate fails because the environment lacks required live FFF capability, record that as an unverified gate and do not close until rerun in the intended environment. If behavior is wrong, reopen the owning implementation bead. Do not add product code, new tests beyond the planned docs-contract assertions, new beads, indexes, SQLite, embeddings, exports, native fields, or managed tools from this checkpoint.

## Exact static gates
From `/data/projects/agent-session-search`:
1. `npm test -- test/readme.test.ts` exits 0; assertions find `days`, `workspace`, `Session Filter`, `Days Filter`, `Workspace Filter`, their distinction from `Project Match`, and `filters_removed_all_results` in the intended docs.
2. `npm run check && npm test && npm run build && npm run smoke && npm run check:dcg` exits 0. Expected: no TypeScript diagnostics; zero failed Vitest suites/tests; build artifacts produced successfully; managed MCP smoke green; DCG validation reports the project guard active/valid.

## Exact live-corpus gates
Use `npm run --silent` so captured stdout remains parseable JSON. Record each command, exit status, and the asserted output.

### 1. Days mtime proof
```zsh
days_json=$(mktemp)
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --days 2 > "$days_json"
jq -e '.metadata.filters.days == 2' "$days_json"
now_epoch=$(date +%s)
while IFS= read -r session_path; do
  session_age=$((now_epoch - $(stat -c %Y -- "$session_path")))
  (( session_age <= 172800 )) || { print -u2 "older than 48h: $session_path"; exit 1; }
done < <(jq -r '.results[]? | .leads[]?.path' "$days_json")
```
Expected: every command exits 0; metadata echoes `days: 2`; no returned lead is older than 172800 seconds.

### 2. Workspace proof
```zsh
workspace_json=$(mktemp)
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --workspace /data/projects/agent-session-search > "$workspace_json"
jq -e '
  [.results[]? | .leads[]?] as $leads
  | .metadata.filters.workspace == "/data/projects/agent-session-search"
    and ([$leads[].source] | unique | length) >= 2
    and any($leads[]; .source == "claude" and (.path | contains("/-data-projects-agent-session-search/")))
    and any($leads[]; .source == "omp" and (.path | contains("/--data-projects-agent-session-search--/")))
    and all($leads[];
      if .source == "claude" then (.path | contains("/-data-projects-agent-session-search/"))
      elif .source == "omp" then (.path | contains("/--data-projects-agent-session-search--/"))
      else true end)
    and all($leads[]; (.path | contains("agent-session-search--worktrees-")) | not)
' "$workspace_json"
jq -r '.results[]? | .leads[]? | [.source,.path] | @tsv' "$workspace_json"
```
Expected: both commands exit 0; canonical workspace is echoed; at least two sources are present; Claude and OMP each contribute their exact workspace segment; the automated assertion rejects every sibling/worktree path (in particular any Claude `-data-projects-agent-session-search--worktrees-...` path), and the printed source/path list is retained as audit evidence.

### 3. Dual-form symlink proof
```zsh
alias_dir=$(mktemp -d)
ln -s /data/projects/agent-session-search "$alias_dir/repo-alias"
canonical_json=$(mktemp)
alias_json=$(mktemp)
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --workspace /data/projects/agent-session-search > "$canonical_json"
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --workspace "$alias_dir/repo-alias" > "$alias_json"
cmp -s "$canonical_json" "$alias_json"
```
Expected: all commands exit 0 and `cmp -s` reports byte-identical output, including the canonical workspace echo.

### 4. Nonexistent workspace warning/exit proof
```zsh
missing_json=$(mktemp)
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --days 3650 --workspace /nonexistent/ws > "$missing_json"
jq -e '
  (.results | length) == 0
  and ([.warnings[] | select(.code == "filters_removed_all_results")] | length) == 1
  and all(.warnings[] | select(.code == "filters_removed_all_results");
    .recommendedAction | type == "string" and length > 0)
' "$missing_json"
```
Expected: CLI exits 0, jq exits 0, results are empty, exactly one `filters_removed_all_results` warning is present, and it includes a non-empty recovery action.

### 5. Typo/error-envelope proof
```zsh
typo_stdout=$(mktemp)
typo_stderr=$(mktemp)
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --dsys 7 >"$typo_stdout" 2>"$typo_stderr"
typo_status=$?
test "$typo_status" -eq 1
test ! -s "$typo_stdout"
jq -e '.error.code == "user_input_error" and (.error.message | contains("did you mean --days?")) and (.error.suggestedCommand | contains("--days 7"))' "$typo_stderr"
```
Expected: the CLI command exits 1, both `test` commands exit 0, stdout is empty, and jq exits 0 after proving the exact user-input error, typo teaching, and copy-pasteable corrected flag.

### 6. Discovery-surface proof
```zsh
npm run --silent dev:cli -- capabilities --json | jq -e '(tostring | contains("--days")) and (tostring | contains("--workspace"))'
npm run --silent dev:cli -- --robot-triage | jq -e '(.recommendedCommands | tostring | contains("--days")) and (.recommendedCommands | tostring | contains("--workspace"))'
```
Expected: both pipelines exit 0; capabilities documents both flags and robot triage contains a filtered example.

### 7. Managed MCP parity + replay proof
Run this exact MCP stdio probe; its initial call is the plan's exact shim call shape, and it echoes one prepared follow-up byte-for-byte under `groupCandidates`:
```zsh
mcp_json=$(mktemp)
node --input-type=module > "$mcp_json" <<'NODE'
import { Client } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js";
import { StdioClientTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js";

const env = Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(process.env).filter((entry) => typeof entry[1] === "string"));
const transport = new StdioClientTransport({
  command: "npm",
  args: ["run", "--silent", "dev:mcp"],
  cwd: process.cwd(),
  env,
  stderr: "pipe",
});
const client = new Client({ name: "filters-live-proof", version: "1.0.0" });
const body = (output) => {
  const text = output.content?.filter((item) => item.type === "text").map((item) => item.text).join("\n");
  if (!text) throw new Error("search_sessions returned no text content");
  return JSON.parse(text);
};
const call = async (args) => body(await client.callTool({ name: "search_sessions", arguments: args }));

try {
  await client.connect(transport);
  const toolNames = (await client.listTools()).tools.map((tool) => tool.name);
  const input = { query: "cass", days: 7, workspace: "/data/projects/agent-session-search", maxResultsPerSource: 5 };
  const initial = await call(input);
  const followup = initial.results?.map((group) => group.more?.groupCandidates).find(Boolean);
  if (!followup) throw new Error("initial response did not prepare more.groupCandidates");
  const replay = await call({ query: "cass", groupCandidates: followup });
  process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ toolNames, initial, replay }));
} finally {
  await client.close();
}
NODE

jq -e '
  .toolNames == ["search_sessions"]
  and .initial.metadata.filters == {"days":7,"workspace":"/data/projects/agent-session-search"}
  and .replay.metadata.filters == {"days":7,"workspace":"/data/projects/agent-session-search"}
  and ([.replay.results[]? | .leads[]?] | length) > 0
' "$mcp_json"

cli_parity_json=$(mktemp)
mcp_paths=$(mktemp)
cli_paths=$(mktemp)
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --days 7 --workspace /data/projects/agent-session-search --max-results 5 > "$cli_parity_json"
jq -S '[.initial.results[]? | .leads[]? | [.source,.path]] | sort' "$mcp_json" > "$mcp_paths"
jq -S '[.results[]? | .leads[]? | [.source,.path]] | sort' "$cli_parity_json" > "$cli_paths"
cmp -s "$mcp_paths" "$cli_paths"

now_epoch=$(date +%s)
while IFS= read -r session_path; do
  session_age=$((now_epoch - $(stat -c %Y -- "$session_path")))
  (( session_age <= 604800 )) || { print -u2 "replay lead older than 7d: $session_path"; exit 1; }
done < <(jq -r '.replay.results[]? | .leads[]?.path' "$mcp_json")
```
Expected: the Node probe and jq exit 0; managed `tools/list` is exactly `["search_sessions"]`; the initial and replay responses echo the exact canonical filters; replay returns at least one filtered lead without a teaching error; `cmp -s` proves MCP and CLI initial source/path lists agree under the same cap; and every replay lead is no older than seven days. Any absent `more.groupCandidates` payload is a failed gate, not a manual exception.

### 8. Native-lane isolation proof
Run the existing smoke and then the exact live schema probe:
```zsh
npm test -- test/native-mcp-smoke.test.ts
native_schema_json=$(mktemp)
node --input-type=module > "$native_schema_json" <<'NODE'
import { Client } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/index.js";
import { StdioClientTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio.js";

const env = Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(process.env).filter((entry) => typeof entry[1] === "string"));
const transport = new StdioClientTransport({
  command: process.execPath,
  args: ["--import", "tsx", "src/native-server.ts"],
  cwd: process.cwd(),
  env,
  stderr: "pipe",
});
const client = new Client({ name: "filters-native-isolation-proof", version: "1.0.0" });
try {
  await client.connect(transport);
  const tools = (await client.listTools()).tools
    .filter((tool) => tool.name === "fff_grep" || tool.name === "fff_multi_grep")
    .map((tool) => ({ name: tool.name, inputSchema: tool.inputSchema }));
  process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(tools));
} finally {
  await client.close();
}
NODE
jq -e '
  ([.[].name] | sort) == ["fff_grep","fff_multi_grep"]
  and all(.[]; ((.inputSchema.properties // {}) | has("days") | not))
  and all(.[]; ((.inputSchema.properties // {}) | has("workspace") | not))
' "$native_schema_json"
```
Expected: the smoke exits 0; on an intended environment with live FFF, the Node probe and jq exit 0, both audited raw FFF tools are present, and neither input schema has `days` or `workspace`. A skipped smoke or unavailable/demoted live tool is recorded as unverified and blocks closing this bead until this exact probe passes on the intended environment.

### 9. Determinism proof
```zsh
first_json=$(mktemp)
second_json=$(mktemp)
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --days 7 --workspace /data/projects/agent-session-search --max-results 5 > "$first_json"
npm run --silent dev:cli -- "cass" --json --days 7 --workspace /data/projects/agent-session-search --max-results 5 > "$second_json"
cmp -s "$first_json" "$second_json"
```
Expected: all commands exit 0 and `cmp -s` proves byte-identical JSON with no response timestamp.
close_reason: Commit ed9d159; docs contract 3/3; check/full suite/build/smoke/DCG green; live gates passed for days mtime, workspace/source isolation, symlink identity, missing-workspace warning, typo envelope, discovery, MCP parity+replay, native isolation, determinism.

title: Add typo-aware CLI flag suggestions

title: Add JSON orphan diagnostics behavior
description:

- Reap mode returns `{ mode: "reap", status, found, reaped, failed }` with success status `passed`.

title: U2b: flatten candidates and shape strict cass robot envelope
description:
U2b — Flatten managed candidates and shape the strict cass 0.6.22 robot response.

PARENT CHECKPOINT: bd-cass-shim-epic-dxz.2 (U2). Depends on U2a (bd-cass-shim-epic-dxz.2.1) and consumes its request/source mapping without redefining it. This child partitions U2's response/lifecycle half; the two children equal U2 exactly. Plan authority: docs/plans/2026-07-20-002-feat-cass-compat-shim-plan.md, U2; R8-R9 and R15; AE2, AE4, AE7; KTD6-KTD8. This is an implementation slice within locked Commit A.

OUTCOME
Managed CandidateGroup results become a deterministic real-cass search envelope whose every hit passes cm 0.2.12's copied zod consumer schema, including required numeric line_number. Partial failures remain parseable successes, total failure is exit 9, and the in-process search instance closes on every path.

OWNED FILES
Complete the result/lifecycle portion of src/cass-compat/search.ts and test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts. Use U2a's agents.ts mapping. No other production files. Negative MCP contract checks run existing test/mcp-smoke.test.ts and test/native-mcp-smoke.test.ts; do not modify MCP code or catalogs.

EXACT TRANSFORMATION
- Flatten CandidateGroup.leads in returned group-priority order, then lead order. Dedupe by source plus canonical absolute path, keeping the first occurrence. Apply the global --limit only after flatten/dedupe. Preserve canonical source_path and attached source identity.
- Stat each selected unique path at most once. Emit created_at as epoch mtimeMs only when grounded; omit it on stat failure without dropping the hit.
- Every hit has source_path:string, agent:string, snippet:string, and line_number:number. Define/export CASS_SHIM_DEFAULT_LINE_NUMBER = 1 and use it whenever lead line is absent; never emit null, undefined, or a numeric string.
- FullHitCompat fields are title (filename stem/sessionId), snippet, content=snippet, monotonic descending ordinal score, source_path, cass-compatible agent from U2a, optional requested-workspace echo, optional created_at, line_number, and match_type/source_id/origin_kind="local". Omit workspace when none was requested; do no dash decoding. Scores are ordinal only.
- Pretty JSON plus trailing newline uses the exact outer keys: query, limit, offset:0, count, total_matches, hits, max_tokens:null, request_id:null, cursor:null, hits_clamped:false. Zero results are exit 0.
- Summarize partial engine/source warnings on stderr while keeping valid stdout parseable and exit 0. A total backend failure yields exit 9, one unknown envelope, and empty stdout; never report it as a successful empty search.
- close?.() executes exactly once in finally for nonempty success, zero-hit success after a constructed search, and thrown/total failure. U2a's unsupported-slug early return constructs no search and therefore closes none.
- Managed MCP discovery remains exactly search_sessions; native discovery remains its existing diagnostic plus policy-approved namespaced FFF tools. No MCP, CLI, cache/index, search-engine rewrite, or shim-local days/workspace filter.

CONFIRMED PUBLIC TEST SEAMS
Exercise the public cass search handler/dispatcher completion with injected createSessionSearch and filesystem-stat boundaries. Assert captured input and returned {stdout, stderr, exitCode}; parse stdout and each hit at this seam. Do not unit-test private flatten/map helpers or mock internal modules. The copied cm schema and literal cass envelope are independent oracles.

MANDATORY TDD ORDER — one vertical slice at a time (RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR)
1. RED FIRST in test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts: "every hit parses with cm 0.2.12's zod schema verbatim". Copy the consumer schema verbatim into the test (not a production/generated type), call .parse on every emitted hit, and observe RED for the absent/incompatible mapping. Add only enough full-hit mapping for green.
2. RED FIRST: "missing lead line emits non-nullable line_number 1". Supply a lead without line; assert the named constant equals 1, output is number 1 (not null/undefined/string), and that same hit passes the copied schema. Green minimally, then refactor.
3. RED FIRST for group/lead ordering, first-wins source+canonical-path dedupe, then global limit. Use independent literal paths/order. Next add a separate stat case proving one stat per selected unique path and grounded/omitted created_at behavior.
4. RED FIRST for exact outer key set, pretty/trailing-newline serialization, workspace echo-or-omit, ordinal score monotonicity, and empty exit-0 response, one behavior per slice.
5. RED FIRST for partial-warning success, then total-backend-failure exit 9, then exactly-once close on nonempty/zero/failure. Do not conflate backend failure with legitimate no matches.
6. RED FIRST negative-catalog gate by running existing MCP smoke contracts; implementation remains unchanged. Rerun all prior slices after every green, then refactor without observable change.

INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION GATES
1. npx vitest run test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts -t "every hit parses with cm 0.2.12's zod schema verbatim" — exit 0; every emitted hit is individually accepted by the copied consumer schema.
2. npx vitest run test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts -t "missing lead line emits non-nullable line_number 1" — exit 0; the output is numeric 1 via CASS_SHIM_DEFAULT_LINE_NUMBER and the hit passes the copied schema.
3. npx vitest run test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts -t "flattens dedupes and limits candidates in contract order" — exit 0; literal expected paths prove group/lead order, first-wins dedupe, and post-dedupe global limit.
4. npx vitest run test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts — exit 0 with one passed test file and zero failed tests; assertions cover exact envelope, canonical paths, stat grounding, warnings/failures, workspace/score semantics, and cleanup.
5. npx vitest run test/mcp-smoke.test.ts test/native-mcp-smoke.test.ts — exit 0 with two passed test files and zero failures; managed tools/list is exactly search_sessions and native discovery matches its existing checked-in policy.
6. npm run check — exit 0 with no TypeScript diagnostics.
7. Inspection: rg -n "CASS_SHIM_DEFAULT_LINE_NUMBER|line_number|createSessionSearch|close|spawn|execFile" src/cass-compat/search.ts test/cass-shim-contract.test.ts — shows numeric fallback and in-process lifecycle, with no package-CLI/real-cass subprocess path.
close_reason: Implemented schema-valid full cass hits, priority/lead flattening, source+path dedupe, post-dedupe limit, one-stat metadata, exact envelope/workspace/scores, warning and total-failure semantics, exact-once cleanup; contract 16/16, MCP gates 9/9, npm check, pre-commit 327/327.

title: U5a: document optional cm interoperability, honesty caveats, and rollback
description:
U5a — Document the optional cass-compatible shim contract and rollback.

Parent scope: bd-cass-shim-epic-dxz.5 (plan Implementation Unit U5).
Plan authority: docs/plans/2026-07-20-002-feat-cass-compat-shim-plan.md, especially R16, AE8, KTD9, KTD11, KTD12, Council resolutions 2-3, and U5's Documentation Contract.
Dependencies: U2, U3, and U4. This child owns only U5's documentation surfaces; U5b owns package.json, src/help.ts, and executable/packed-install tests.

OUTCOME
Make the shim's complete six-surface cm interoperability contract understandable, explicitly optional, version-pinned, diagnostically honest, and reversible without presenting it as a CLI subcommand, third MCP lane, general export product, cass repair, or persistent migration.

OWNED FILES — EXACT PARTITION
- Create docs/cass-shim.md.
- Modify DESIGN.md.
- Modify README.md.
- Modify CONTEXT.md.
Do not modify package.json, src/help.ts, test/packaging.test.ts, runtime modules, MCP modules, docs/cli.md, or Beads outside this parent/child unit.

DOCS/CASS-SHIM.MD CONTRACT
The reference document must be self-contained and cover:
1. Identity and scope: agent-session-search-cass-shim is an optional cm subprocess compatibility adapter backed by live/index-free FFF search, not a CLI subcommand, MCP tool/server, cass passthrough, or index repair.
2. Pinned consumer baseline: cm 0.2.12 (2026-07-19 build) and real cass 0.6.22. State that argv, schemas, and consumption heuristics must be re-verified on every cm upgrade; do not claim the deferred verify-cm-contract script exists.
3. Supported surfaces and exact supported flags: --version, health, search, export, timeline, stats; cm-used -- separators; repeated --agent; --fields; --robot; --days; --workspace; --since 7d/bare positive integer; --json; export markdown/text. Unsupported commands do not pass through.
4. Stdout/stderr/exit contract: success data only on stdout; warnings/errors on stderr; exit 0/2/4/9 meanings; the shim never emits cass index-missing 3 or timeout 10.
5. Search JSON/hit reference and cass slug mapping, including claude_code↔claude and pi_agent↔pi; numeric line_number fallback; workspace echo-or-omit; grounded created_at only; synthetic score is a monotonic ordinal, not a relevance score and not comparable with cass.
6. Timeline/stats honesty: UTC-hour grouping, newest-1000 timeline cap, message_count:0 deliberately because pinned cm consumes path+agent, stats per-root 5000 cap, messages:null/not computed, truncation disclosure, shim marker, and live/no-index semantics.
7. Export exception: bounded cm-only markdown/text rendering, never an UNKNOWN role header, and exit 9 fallback for empty/unparseable/oversized input. State this is the narrow exception to DESIGN.md's markdown-export Non-Goal, not a reusable transcript-export API.
8. Activation: show an environment-scoped CASS_PATH invocation and the supported cassPath config option. Make clear that live acceptance uses only environment prefixes and does not rewrite config.
9. Diagnostics: --version, health, built search/timeline/stats/export examples, cm doctor/context/reflect dry-run checks, and the expected shim marker/no-index explanation.
10. Rollback: environment activation rolls back by removing/unsetting CASS_PATH; config activation rolls back by removing/restoring cassPath. No session migration, cache, index, or package-configuration rewrite is required.
11. Documentation-only degraded note: backporting ahead of the predecessor days/workspace filters loses required filter fidelity; there is no shim-local post-filter or supported degraded implementation.

OTHER DOCUMENT SURFACES
- DESIGN.md Product Contract gains the fifth bin, naming agent-session-search-cass-shim as optional cm interoperability. Its markdown-export Non-Goal is narrowly annotated with the shim-only adapter exception; the general Non-Goal remains intact.
- README.md gains a short optional cm-interoperability section linking to docs/cass-shim.md. It continues to state that search_sessions is the only managed MCP tool and does not turn the shim into the primary product.
- CONTEXT.md gains one concise Key Modules/entrypoints line for src/cass-shim.ts and src/cass-compat/, while preserving the FFF-as-core and two-lane boundaries.

PURE-DOCS TDD SUBSTITUTE — RED → GREEN → REVIEW
No production behavior or test harness belongs to this child, so the verification substitute is an executable contract audit.

GREEN:

REVIEW/REFACTOR:

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

PRE-AGREED PUBLIC TEST SEAMS
Use the package boundary already established by test/packaging.test.ts: clean build, npm pack file manifest, install into a temporary app, invoke installed .bin processes, parse capabilities JSON, and connect to installed MCP binaries. Assert observable files, permissions, stdout, stderr, exit codes, tool names, and monotonic elapsed time. Do not import private packaging helpers, mock internal cass-compat modules, or verify implementation call order.

MANDATORY TDD — RED → GREEN → REVIEW/REFACTOR, ONE VERTICAL SLICE AT A TIME
All new automated assertions live in test/packaging.test.ts. Extend the existing public package-boundary test titled "ships executable bin entries without local agent files or tests" so the expensive pack/install fixture remains one coherent seam; do not duplicate tarball installations merely to isolate assertions. For each slice, add only that slice's assertions first, run the exact existing-title -t command, and observe a behavior assertion fail for the missing bin/files/capability (not a fixture/setup failure); make only the smallest owned-file change to pass; rerun green before moving on.

GREEN: fix only packaging wiring or an owner-scoped packaging defect; handler behavior belongs to U1.
3. Slice 3 — separate capabilities entrypoint and unchanged managed tool contract
   RED: parse capabilities --json; assert mcp.tools.map(name) is exactly ["search_sessions"], assert the separate optional shim entrypoint command/optIn values, and assert the shim name is absent from mcp.tools. GREEN: update only src/help.ts.

After all slices are green, review/refactor only the three owned files, then rerun all gates. No production refactor beyond package metadata/capabilities is authorized.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

title: fix: groupCandidates replay loses symlink-alias workspace form
description: MAJOR from plan-council review (reviewer-1, reproduced end-to-end; full gate suite was green — this is a semantic hole tests missed). Defect: searchSessions prepares dual-form workspace filters (resolved-absolute + realpath) per plan D1, but replaces effectiveInput.workspace with ONLY the canonical realpath before building more.groupCandidates (src/search.ts ~:102-108, ~:975-976). Page 1 matches sessions under the dash-encoded SYMLINK ALIAS form; replay re-derives filters from realpath alone, loses the alias encoded form, drops valid candidates, and returns [] + filters_removed_all_results for the same query. Repro: symlink workspace alias -> alias-encoded session dir -> page 1 has hits -> replay via more.groupCandidates -> empty. FIX DIRECTION (decide in-bead): carry the resolved-absolute (pre-realpath) form in the followup payload so replay deterministically re-derives BOTH forms, OR carry both forms explicitly; either way fingerprint determinism, mismatch validation, and filter-less payload stability MUST be preserved (pinned-fingerprint test must still pass unchanged for filter-less payloads). TDD: FIRST add the reviewer's repro as a failing test in test/search.test.ts (symlink workspace via mkdtemp+ln -s, alias-encoded session dir, assert page1 hits AND replay parity), then fix. Gates: npm run check green; focused vitest test/search.test.ts + test/tool.test.ts green; new symlink-replay test red-before/green-after; existing pinned fingerprint literal for filter-less payloads unchanged. Scope: src/search.ts (+ possibly src/session-filters.ts export), tests. Plan ref: docs/plans/2026-07-20-002-feat-days-workspace-filters-plan.md (D1 dual-form + decision 5 replay carry).

title: fix: canonicalProjectPath can return relative paths and mis-resolves missing HOME
description: Found (not fixed) by the filters simplification applier while comparing canonicalization helpers. PRE-EXISTING defect in the ranking lane, unrelated to the days/workspace filters. src/search.ts canonicalProjectPath: (1) after certain realpath failures it returns a RELATIVE path instead of an absolute one, so downstream pathIsWithin/containment comparisons against absolute candidate paths silently fail to match; (2) with HOME unset, a '~' input resolves to '.' rather than erroring or being left literal. Impact is ranking-only (project-match boost may be silently skipped) — it is NOT a filter-correctness bug, because src/session-filters.ts canonicalWorkspacePath is a separate implementation that always normalizes to an absolute path (verified: the two helpers are deliberately NOT interchangeable — they differ in home expansion, relative-path fallback, symlink-form exposure, and missing-path handling; an independent verifier agent confirmed leaving them separate was correct). Do NOT unify with canonicalWorkspacePath — that was adjudicated and rejected. Gates: npm run check; focused vitest test/search.test.ts green; ranking behavior for currently-working inputs must remain identical.
